title: Keller Williams vs Redfin: Which is Best for Realtors in 2026?

content:
This article will provide a comprehensive comparison between Keller Williams vs Redfin to help you determine which brokerage better aligns with your professional goals. Choosing the right brokerage is a critical decision for any real estate agent, as it significantly impacts their career trajectory and financial success.

Commission Splits and Fees

The commission splits and associated fees are crucial factors to consider when selecting a brokerage. Keller Williams offers flexible splits ranging from 70/30 to 100/0, with a 6% franchise/royalty fee until the cap is reached. In contrast, Redfin provides splits starting at 30/70 (with a base salary) and going up to 75/25, with no franchise/royalty fee.

Monthly fees also differ significantly. Keller Williams’ fees vary by office, whereas Redfin has no monthly fees. Additionally, Keller Williams does not charge transaction fees, while Redfin also does not charge transaction fees. These differences can impact an agent’s net income and financial planning, making it important to consider each brokerage’s fee structure.

Income Opportunities

The potential for additional income streams can significantly enhance an agent’s financial stability and growth. Keller Williams stands out by offering revenue share programs that provide a retirement and willable income path. These additional income streams can offer substantial long-term financial benefits and security. Conversely, Redfin does not offer such supplementary income opportunities, limiting agents primarily to their commission-based earnings.

Training and Support

Effective training and support are vital for both new and experienced agents. Keller Williams provides extensive training opportunities, though specific details on the extent of their offerings are not standardized across all offices. In contrast, Redfin offers four weeks of local training, which might be beneficial for hands-on learning. Neither brokerage offers 24/7 support, which can be crucial for agents who require ongoing assistance and professional development.

Technology and Resources

In the modern real estate landscape, leveraging technology effectively is essential. Keller Williams is known for its robust technology platforms, offering tools that enhance productivity and client engagement. On the other hand, Redfin is also known for its technology-driven approach, providing agents with comprehensive tools and resources. Both brokerages provide valuable technological resources, but the specific tools and platforms offered may appeal differently depending on the agent’s needs.

Agent Satisfaction

Agent satisfaction is a key indicator of a brokerage’s effectiveness and overall agent happiness. Keller Williams boasts a high Glassdoor rating of 4.3 stars based on 7.9K reviews, indicating strong agent approval and satisfaction. Redfin holds a lower rating of 3.6 stars from 1.9K reviews, suggesting that there may be areas needing improvement. The differences in agent satisfaction can often be attributed to the varying levels of support, training, and income opportunities provided by each brokerage.

Final Thoughts on Keller Williams vs Redfin

In comparing Keller Williams and Redfin, it’s evident that each brokerage has its strengths and weaknesses. Keller Williams offers more favorable commission splits, extensive training, and additional income opportunities, making it a compelling choice for both new and experienced agents. The innovative technology and comprehensive support further enhance Keller Williams’ appeal. Redfin, with its no-fee structure, agent salary option, and comprehensive local training, may attract agents looking for a cost-effective and hands-on learning environment.

For a more detailed comparison and to find the best brokerage for your needs, check out our brokerage comparison tool.

When making your decision, consider your specific needs and career objectives. Evaluate the advantages and disadvantages carefully to choose the brokerage that best fits your professional aspirations. Whether you prioritize comprehensive support and training or seek a brokerage with a more conventional setup, your decision should align with your long-term career goals and preferences.

title: Best Online Texas Real Estate Schools
excerpt: Choosing one of the best online Texas real estate school is crucial for your career, and it’s important to thoroughly research your options and understand…
content:
Choosing one of the best online Texas real estate school is crucial for your career, and it’s important to thoroughly research your options and understand what each course offers. While there are countless real estate schools available, only a select few stand out for their exceptional education and comprehensive support in preparing you to obtain your real estate license.

At Smart Agent Alliance, we are committed to guiding new agents in launching successful careers. That’s why we’ve curated a list of the leading online real estate schools in Texas. Our detailed analysis covers the offerings of each school, their course delivery methods, and a balanced view of the advantages and disadvantages of studying with each one.

Table of Contents

  1. AceableAgent
  2. The CE Shop
  3. Colibri Real Estate
  4. RealEstateU
  5. 360 Training

AceableAgent

SmartAgentAlliance Score: A+
Top Feature: Ability to study on the go with the mobile app

Why We Chose It:

AceableAgent has quickly become a favorite among students due to its modern approach and flexibility. It is exclusively focused on real estate education, offering courses for every step of a real estate career, from pre-licensing to continuing education and career advancement. The courses are state-approved and designed to be accessible from any device, providing students with the flexibility to study anywhere.

Overview:

AceableAgent offers three pre-licensing packages, all of which include the required coursework. The courses are fully online and accessible via desktop, laptop, tablet, or smartphone. This accessibility is one of AceableAgent’s standout features, allowing students to study on the go. Courses are developed by experienced professionals and are accredited by ARELLO. Additionally, AceableAgent is known for its interactive coursework, which helps students grasp and retain information effectively.

Why AceableAgent is Great for Pre-Licensing:

AceableAgent’s pre-licensing courses are available in multiple states, ensuring they meet state-specific requirements. The courses include engaging video lectures, interactive content, and practice exams to help students prepare effectively for their licensing exams. AceableAgent offers a satisfaction guarantee and provides career resources to help new agents start their careers successfully.

User Experiences and Feedback:

Students have reported that AceableAgent’s courses are particularly user-friendly, with a modern interface that makes navigation easy. The mobile app is highly rated for its convenience, allowing users to study during commutes or breaks. The interactive content, including quizzes and video explanations, has been praised for making complex topics easier to understand. Additionally, AceableAgent’s customer service, though sometimes slow, is generally helpful and responsive to student needs.

Pre-Licensing

Price Starts at: $449
Basic Package Features: 180-Hour Package: Six 30-hour online courses, email instructor support, narrated lessons, 1,000 exam practice questions, and a mini-course on finding a sponsoring broker.
Upgrade Packages: Yes, the upgraded packages offer Virtual Instructor: AI-powered, available 24/7 to answer questions and explain concepts. Includes 3 months of PrepAgent exam prep with 75 videos, 70 audio lessons, and 1,660+ practice questions. Offers live tutoring, webinars 5 times a week, and live Q&A sessions.

Exam Prep

Price Starts at: $59
Basic Package Features: Access to Prep Agent with Thousands of practice questions; 100% money-back guarantee; 75+ real estate exam prep videos; online interactive flashcards; 100+ page exam prep ebook; hours of audio lessons; interactive vocabulary worksheet; 100s of hours of recorded webinars; live online webinars.
Upgrade Packages: Yes, the upgraded packages offer access to PrepAgent for 1 or 3 months, compared to just 1 week with the basic plan.

Continuing Education

Price Starts at: $89
Basic Package Features: There are a bunch of separate ones but the main 18-hour package includes TREC-Approved 18-Hour Course Package: Five courses, all in one place, covering 2024-2025 requirements. Start immediately with no classroom needed. Engaging, up-to-date content available anytime.
Upgrade Packages: None

Flexible Learning Options: Enjoy self-paced courses that fit your schedule.

Extended Course Access: Access course materials for up to six months from your enrollment date.

Risk-Free Enrollment: Benefit from a 30-day money-back guarantee if you’re not satisfied.

Exam Success Guarantee: Pass the licensing exam within three tries or receive a full course refund.

Comprehensive Exam Preparation: Gain access to a variety of study tools including prep videos, 75 audio lessons, digital flashcards, over 1600 practice questions, a 100-page exam prep ebook, weekly live webinars, live Q&A sessions with instructors, and the option for personalized 1-on-1 tutoring.

Dedicated Support: Instructor assistance is available seven days a week via email, and customer service can be reached by email and phone from Monday to Friday.

Convenient Final Exams: Utilize online proctoring services for the final exam across the nation, although California students do not need a proctor for their pre-licensing exam.

Mobile-Friendly Platform: Study anytime, anywhere with a course platform that is fully optimized for mobile devices.

Interactive Learning Tools: Engage with interactive content designed to enhance your learning experience and retention.

Career Resources: Access additional career support services to help you transition from student to professional agent successfully.

Pros:

  • Mobile app for studying
  • High pass rates among schools with 1,000+ students
  • Engaging and entertaining course materials
  • Exam prep through PrepAgent

Cons:

  • Course access lasts for 12 months
  • Some bugs reported in the mobile app
  • Limited technical support
  • Outsourced exam proctoring

The CE Shop

SmartAgentAlliance Score: A
Top Feature: Course is easy to follow, high pass rates among students

Why We Chose It: The CE Shop offers a variety of real estate education options with an interactive and inquiry-based approach in their studying methods. While their focus is mainly on continuing education, they also provide excellent pre-licensing courses, exam prep, and post-licensing education. Their courses are accredited by ARELLO and have received numerous awards for their educational programs.

Overview: The CE Shop’s pre-licensing courses can be taken in 34 states, through four different course packages: Courses Only, Standard Package, Value Package, and Premium Package. Every package comes with business ebooks, career resources, downloadable resources, real estate glossary, digital flashcards, study schedules, and access to instructor-led webinars. Students have praised the course content for being easy to follow and highly informative, which has led to high pass rates.

Why The CE Shop is Great for Pre-Licensing: The CE Shop’s pre-licensing courses emphasize real-world applications with scenario-based learning. This approach helps students apply their knowledge to practical situations they will face in their real estate careers. Additionally, The CE Shop provides an exam prep edge program that offers additional practice tests and study aids to ensure students are well-prepared for their licensing exams.

User Experiences and Feedback: The CE Shop is well-regarded for its robust customer support and comprehensive resources. The ability to access instructor-led webinars provides an additional layer of support and interaction, which can be particularly beneficial for students who need more guidance. The frequent promotions and discounts make it a cost-effective option for many. Students appreciate the clear and structured study schedules, which help them stay on track and complete the course efficiently.

Upgrade Packages: ProPath Real Estate Career Kickstarter: Includes the “Real Estate Basics and Beyond” eTextbook and three actionable courses: 30/60/90 Day Plan, Core Lead Network, and Negotiation Skills. Total of 9 hours of professional development to kickstart your real estate career, though it doesn’t count towards CE credits.

Basic Package Features: Texas SAE Post-Licensing Package: A 90-hour program fulfilling mandatory requirements for the first renewal cycle, excluding Texas Legal Update 1 & 2. Includes three 30-hour courses: Real Estate Appraisal, Real Estate Investment, and Real Estate Brokerage.
Upgrade Packages: Yes, The ProPath Real Estate Business Builder offers three key courses: Pricing Strategies for mastering home pricing, Tax Planning for managing finances and choosing retirement plans, and Budgeting to estimate earnings and plan for taxes. It’s designed to set you up for success in real estate.

Continuing Education

Basic Package Features: This 18-hour package meets Texas license renewal requirements with 11 mandatory and 7 elective hours. It includes courses on Texas Legal Updates I & II (4 hours each), Document Diligence (3 mandatory, 1 elective hour), Ethical Dilemmas (3 elective hours), and Investor Strategies (3 elective hours).

Pass Assurance: Exam cost reimbursed if you don’t pass on the first attempt.

Support Services: Available daily via email, phone, or chat.

Final Exam Procedure: Online proctoring available nationwide; California exams are administered by the DRE at specific centers.

  • Interactive and easy-to-follow course material

Our Review Process:

At SmartAgentAlliance, our goal is to provide accurate and helpful reviews of online real estate schools to assist prospective real estate agents in making informed decisions. Our review process involves several key steps:

Research and Data Collection:

We begin by gathering comprehensive information about each real estate school, including course offerings, pricing, accreditation, and user reviews. This involves visiting the schools’ websites, reading third-party reviews, and gathering feedback from current and former students.

Evaluation Criteria:

Our evaluation is based on several important criteria, including:

  • Course Quality: We assess the content and structure of the courses to ensure they cover all necessary topics comprehensively and are easy to follow.
  • Accessibility: We evaluate the flexibility and accessibility of the courses, including the availability of mobile apps and study materials.
  • Pass Rates: We consider the pass rates of students who have completed the courses and taken the licensing exam.
  • Student Support: We look at the level of support provided to students, including access to instructors, customer service, and additional resources like study guides and practice exams.
  • Pricing: We compare the cost of the courses relative to the value they offer, including any discounts or payment plans available.
  • User Feedback: We gather and analyze feedback from students who have taken the courses to get an understanding of their experiences and satisfaction levels.

Scoring and Ranking:

Each school is scored based on the evaluation criteria, and an overall score is calculated. The scores are then used to rank the schools, with additional considerations given to standout features that may set a school apart from the rest.

title: How to Get a Real Estate License in Texas: 2026 Guide
excerpt: Getting your Texas real estate license might seem daunting, but with the right guidance, it’s a straightforward process. This comprehensive guide will walk you through…
content:
Getting your Texas real estate license might seem daunting, but with the right guidance, it’s a straightforward process. This comprehensive guide will walk you through each step, ensuring you’re well-prepared to start your real estate career in Texas. Let’s get started on your journey to becoming a licensed real estate agent.

Table of Contents

  1. Basic Requirements
  2. Choosing a Real Estate School

Basic Requirements

Before getting into the specific steps, it’s important to understand the fundamental requirements for obtaining a Texas real estate license. Here’s what you need to know:

Applicants must be at least 18 years old to apply for a real estate license in Texas. This age requirement ensures that applicants have reached a level of maturity and legal responsibility needed for the profession.

excerpt: Choosing one of the best online real estate schools in Florida is crucial for your career, and it’s important to thoroughly research your options and…
content:
Choosing one of the best online real estate schools in Florida is crucial for your career, and it’s important to thoroughly research your options and understand what each course offers. While there are countless real estate schools available, only a select few stand out for their exceptional education and comprehensive support in preparing you to obtain your real estate license.

At Smart Agent Alliance, we are committed to guiding new agents in launching successful careers. That’s why we’ve curated a list of the leading online real estate schools in Florida. Our detailed analysis covers the offerings of each school, their course delivery methods, and a balanced view of the advantages and disadvantages of studying with each one.

Table of Contents

  1. AceableAgent
  2. The CE Shop
  3. Colibri Real Estate
  4. RealEstateU
  5. 360 Training

AceableAgent

SmartAgentAlliance Score: A+
Top Feature: Ability to study on the go with the mobile app

Why We Chose It:

AceableAgent has quickly become a favorite among students due to its modern approach and flexibility. It is exclusively focused on real estate education, offering courses for every step of a real estate career, from pre-licensing to continuing education and career advancement. The courses are state-approved and designed to be accessible from any device, providing students with the flexibility to study anywhere.

Overview:

AceableAgent offers three pre-licensing packages, all of which include the required coursework. The courses are fully online and accessible via desktop, laptop, tablet, or smartphone. This accessibility is one of AceableAgent’s standout features, allowing students to study on the go. Courses are developed by experienced professionals and are accredited by ARELLO. Additionally, AceableAgent is known for its interactive coursework, which helps students grasp and retain information effectively.

Why AceableAgent is Great for Pre-Licensing:

AceableAgent’s pre-licensing courses are available in multiple states, ensuring they meet state-specific requirements. The courses include engaging video lectures, interactive content, and practice exams to help students prepare effectively for their licensing exams. AceableAgent offers a satisfaction guarantee and provides career resources to help new agents start their careers successfully.

User Experiences and Feedback:

Students have reported that AceableAgent’s courses are particularly user-friendly, with a modern interface that makes navigation easy. The mobile app is highly rated for its convenience, allowing users to study during commutes or breaks. The interactive content, including quizzes and video explanations, has been praised for making complex topics easier to understand. Additionally, AceableAgent’s customer service, though sometimes slow, is generally helpful and responsive to student needs.

Pre-Licensing

Price Starts at: $149
Basic Package Features: The AceableAgent 63-Hour Course includes instructor support via email; fully narrated courses with audio guides; Lite Exam Prep with 1,000 unique practice questions; a mini-course to help you find the right sponsoring broker as you start your real estate career.
Upgrade Packages: Yes, the upgraded packages offer 3 months of access, 75 videos, 70 audio lessons, and digital flashcards. It also includes 1,660+ practice questions, a 100+ page exam prep ebook, 1 hour of private tutoring, live webinars five times a week, and live Q&A sessions with expert tutors.

Exam Prep

Price Starts at: $59
Basic Package Features: Thousands of practice questions; 100% money-back guarantee; 75+ real estate exam prep videos; online interactive flashcards; 100+ page exam prep ebook; hours of audio lessons; interactive vocabulary worksheet; 100s of hours of recorded webinars; live online webinars.
Upgrade Packages: Yes, the upgraded packages offer access to PrepAgent for 1 or 2 months, compared to just 1 week with the basic plan.

Flexible Learning Options: Enjoy self-paced courses that fit your schedule.

Extended Course Access: Access course materials for up to six months from your enrollment date.

Risk-Free Enrollment: Benefit from a 30-day money-back guarantee if you’re not satisfied.

Exam Success Guarantee: Pass the licensing exam within three tries or receive a full course refund.

Comprehensive Exam Preparation: Gain access to a variety of study tools including prep videos, 75 audio lessons, digital flashcards, over 1600 practice questions, a 100-page exam prep ebook, weekly live webinars, live Q&A sessions with instructors, and the option for personalized 1-on-1 tutoring.

Dedicated Support: Instructor assistance is available seven days a week via email, and customer service can be reached by email and phone from Monday to Friday.

Convenient Final Exams: Utilize online proctoring services for the final exam across the nation, although California students do not need a proctor for their pre-licensing exam.

Mobile-Friendly Platform: Study anytime, anywhere with a course platform that is fully optimized for mobile devices.

Interactive Learning Tools: Engage with interactive content designed to enhance your learning experience and retention.

Career Resources: Access additional career support services to help you transition from student to professional agent successfully.

Pros:

  • Mobile app for studying
  • High pass rates among schools with 1,000+ students
  • Engaging and entertaining course materials
  • Exam prep through PrepAgent

Cons:

  • Course access lasts for 12 months
  • Some bugs reported in the mobile app
  • Limited technical support
  • Outsourced exam proctoring

To view their current pricing for pre-licensing click this linked button. If you are interested in any of the other courses they offer, you can find that through this link as well.

The CE Shop

SmartAgentAlliance Score: A
Top Feature: Course is easy to follow, high pass rates among students

Why We Chose It: The CE Shop offers a variety of real estate education options with an interactive and inquiry-based approach in their studying methods. While their focus is mainly on continuing education, they also provide excellent pre-licensing courses, exam prep, and post-licensing education. Their courses are accredited by ARELLO and have received numerous awards for their educational programs.

Overview: The CE Shop’s pre-licensing courses can be taken in 34 states, through four different course packages: Courses Only, Standard Package, Value Package, and Premium Package. Every package comes with business ebooks, career resources, downloadable resources, real estate glossary, digital flashcards, study schedules, and access to instructor-led webinars. Students have praised the course content for being easy to follow and highly informative, which has led to high pass rates.

Why The CE Shop is Great for Pre-Licensing: The CE Shop’s pre-licensing courses emphasize real-world applications with scenario-based learning. This approach helps students apply their knowledge to practical situations they will face in their real estate careers. Additionally, The CE Shop provides an exam prep edge program that offers additional practice tests and study aids to ensure students are well-prepared for their licensing exams.

User Experiences and Feedback: The CE Shop is well-regarded for its robust customer support and comprehensive resources. The ability to access instructor-led webinars provides an additional layer of support and interaction, which can be particularly beneficial for students who need more guidance. The frequent promotions and discounts make it a cost-effective option for many. Students appreciate the clear and structured study schedules, which help them stay on track and complete the course efficiently.

Pre-Licensing

Price Starts at: $111.20

Basic Package Features: This package includes the 63-Hour FL Real Estate Sales Associate Licensing Course, which prepares you for the Florida state licensing exam and equips you with the essential business knowledge to succeed as a real estate salesperson in Florida.

Upgrade Packages: The Premium Package includes the eTextbook *Real Estate Basics and Beyond*, FL Exam Prep Edge, and your first license renewal package for free. Additionally, the 2024 Real Estate Success Builder program offers courses in performance improvement, time management, and market insights.

Exam Prep

Price Starts at: $101.25

Basic Package Features: Offers content based on the Florida Candidate Information Booklet, covers every topic for your licensing exam. Exam Prep Edge provides individual lessons by topic for efficient studying, with real-time gauges to track your readiness. We offer 7-day support through call, chat, or email for any questions.

Upgrade Packages: None

Post-Licensing

Price Starts at: $105

Basic Package Features: The Florida Post-Licensing package includes 45 hours of courses required for Sales Associate licensees in their first renewal cycle. These courses must be completed before the license’s first renewal expiration date and include 15 hours each in Business Planning, Finance & Economics, and Market Specialties.

Upgrade Packages: Yes, this package includes the ProPath Real Estate Business Builder program, covering Pricing Strategies, Tax Planning for the Self-Employed, and Budgeting to Build Your Business.

Continuing Education

Price Starts at: $39

Basic Package Features: This package provides all 14 hours needed for active license renewals, including Florida Core Law (3 hours), Document Diligence: Safeguarding Your Transactions (4 hours), Using the Code to Solve Ethical Dilemmas (3 hours), and Assistance Animals and Fair Housing (4 hours).

Upgrade Packages: Yes, this package includes all 14 hours needed for Florida real estate license renewal and the ProPath Network Builder program, which focuses on enhancing your networking skills, creating value propositions, and building long-term business relationships to boost your real estate career.

Learning Format: Flexible, self-paced online courses.

Access Duration: Six months from the start date.

Money-Back Guarantee: Full refund within 30 days if less than 50% of the course is completed.

Pass Assurance: Exam cost reimbursed if you don’t pass on the first attempt.

Exam Preparation: Targeted review sessions, custom study plans, unlimited practice tests, and live webinars.

Support Services: Available daily via email, phone, or chat.

Final Exam Procedure: Online proctoring available nationwide; California exams are administered by the DRE at specific centers.

Extra Resources: Includes digital flashcards, a real estate glossary, a study schedule, business courses, and state-specific content.

Pros:

  • Multiple study package options and monthly payment plans
  • High pass rates among students
  • Interactive and easy-to-follow course material
  • Frequent discounts and promotions

Cons:

  • Mainly text-based study materials
  • Some students find the materials take longer to complete than expected
  • Limited coverage of real estate math

Colibri Real Estate

SmartAgentAlliance Score: A
Top Feature: Affordable and comprehensive study materials

Why We Chose It: Colibri Real Estate is known for its affordability and thorough course materials. It provides excellent support to students preparing for their exams and offers a range of courses that cater to different learning needs. Colibri’s courses are designed to be clear and easy to understand, making them a great choice for budget-conscious students.

Overview: Colibri Real Estate offers various online courses with comprehensive study materials. The courses are designed to prepare students effectively for their licensing exams, with detailed support and resources. The pricing is competitive, making it an accessible option for many students.

Why Colibri Real Estate is Great for Pre-Licensing: Colibri Real Estate’ pre-licensing courses include interactive study tools such as flashcards, practice tests, and exam simulators. The courses are state-approved and designed to meet all the requirements for licensure. Colibri also offers a “pass or don’t pay” guarantee, ensuring students can retake the course if they don’t pass the exam on the first try. They also provide a career starter guide to help new agents transition smoothly into their real estate careers.

User Experiences and Feedback: Colibri Real Estate has a strong reputation for providing value for money. The comprehensive study materials are frequently updated to reflect the latest industry standards and practices. Students have noted that the detailed exam preparation support, including practice exams and study guides, is particularly helpful for passing the state licensing exams. The school’s customer support, though sometimes slow, is knowledgeable and helpful in resolving issues.

Pre-Licensing

Price Starts at: $88.20
Basic Package Features: The Pre-License Course covers the state-required 63 hours, offering flexible audio learning and access to local experts. Retain key concepts with three e-books: “Fearless Math,” “Quick Start Guide,” and the course manual.
Upgrade Packages: CompuCram Exam Prep, included in The Career Essentials and Ultimate packages, offers flashcards, dynamic feedback, unlimited practice tests, and simulated exams. It’s mobile-friendly and includes live Exam Crammer webinars, audible course summaries, a 1-year Career Booster Pack, and a course textbook.

Exam Prep

Price Starts at: $69
Basic Package Features: Exam Prep with audio playback lets you review key topics on the go. If you don’t pass on the first try, you get a refund with the Pass or Don’t Pay Guarantee.
Upgrade Packages: Yes, their packages include 40 hours of live Q&A sessions, a 6-part Live Exam Crammer Series, and a Pass or Don’t Pay Guarantee (excludes FL, NJ, TN). The Post-License Course is self-paced, letting you complete required education on your schedule.

Post-Licensing

Price Starts at: $87.20
Basic Package Features: The 45-hour Post-License Course offers self-paced online learning with access to expert instructors. It includes a RISMedia Premier Membership and audio course materials.
Upgrade Packages: None

Continuing Education

Price Starts at: $87.20
Basic Package Features: The 45-hour Post-License Course is self-paced, allowing you to complete the required education online. It includes access to expert local instructors, RISMedia Premier Membership benefits, and an audio learning option for flexibility.
Upgrade Packages: None

Learning Options: Choose between self-paced online courses or live-streamed classes with real-time instructors for a classroom-like experience from home.

Course Access: Six months of access from the enrollment date.

Refund Policy: Full refund for self-paced courses if canceled within 30 days, and within 24 hours for live-stream courses.

Pass Guarantee: For packages with exam prep, Colibri will refund the course cost if you score 80% or higher on simulated exams but don’t pass the state exam on the first try.

Exam Preparation: Includes digital flashcards, mock exams, readiness assessments, audio review guides, live Q&A sessions, and six one-hour live webinars with instructors for focused preparation.

Instructor Support: Available by phone during business hours or via email for additional assistance.

Final Exam Process: Virtual proctoring available nationwide; in California, the DRE administers the exam at designated centers.

Additional Resources: Access to state-specific pre-licensing, continuing education, broker licensing, and mortgage licensing courses, as well as practice exams and a real estate dictionary.

Pros:

  • Affordable courses
  • Comprehensive study materials
  • Detailed exam preparation support

Cons:

  • Limited interactivity in course materials
  • Some students found customer support to be slow

RealEstateU

SmartAgentAlliance Score: A-
Top Feature: Best affordable courses available

Why We Chose It: RealEstateU is known for its affordability and straightforward approach to real estate education. The audio-based courses are perfect for students who prefer to study on the go. The courses are designed to be simple yet effective, making them an excellent choice for budget-conscious students.

Overview: RealEstateU offers audio lectures that can be accessed on a computer, smartphone, or tablet. The courses are divided into chapters with related key terms, infographics, and quizzes to reinforce learning. Students can take the final exam online and receive a certificate of completion upon passing. The courses are highly affordable, often available at discounted rates.

Why RealEstateU is Great for Pre-Licensing: RealEstateU’s pre-licensing courses are tailored for various states, ensuring compliance with state-specific regulations. The audio-based format is ideal for students who prefer auditory learning, and the courses are designed to be flexible and convenient. RealEstateU also offers an online exam prep course to help students prepare for their licensing exam and includes a detailed course completion timeline to keep students on track.

User Experiences and Feedback: RealEstateU’s unique audio-based approach allows students to study hands-free, making it convenient for those with busy schedules. The courses are structured to be easy to follow, with clear explanations and relevant examples. Students appreciate the flexibility to listen to lectures while commuting or during other activities. The affordable pricing, coupled with frequent discounts, makes RealEstateU an attractive option for many.

Pre-Licensing

Price Starts at: $69
Basic Package Features: Includes the state-required 63 hours of training and allows you to study at your own pace with on-demand lessons. If you’re self-motivated, this course provides everything you need to prepare for your exam.
Upgrade Packages: The “Course + Study Guide” package adds The State Exam Study Guide, which reduces study time by up to 90% by focusing on essential information. It includes sample questions, 16 practice exams, and an e-textbook with all course content, key terms, infographics, and supporting documents. The e-textbook allows offline access, and you can print and highlight key lessons for easy review on the go.

Exam Prep

Price Starts at: $79
Basic Package Features: RealEstateU’s Florida Real Estate Test Prep includes a study guide specifically designed for the Florida real estate license exam and three practice tests to help you prepare effectively.
Upgrade Packages: None

Learning Formats: RealEstateU offers self-paced online courses with audio lessons and downloadable materials to help you learn at your own convenience. The courses are accessible on any device, making it easy to study anytime, anywhere.

Course Access: Courses are available for 12 months from the date of enrollment, giving you ample time to complete your studies.

Refund Policy: They offer a 30-day satisfaction guarantee. If you complete less than 50% of the course and are not satisfied, you can request a refund within this period.

Exam Preparation: The courses include state exam study guides, sample questions, practice exams, and an “Agent Success Course” to help you prepare thoroughly for the licensing exam.

Student Support: 24/7 customer support is available via email to assist with any questions or issues.

Additional Resources: Infographic materials, e-textbooks, and detailed lesson outlines to enhance learning. They also offer specialized real estate investment courses covering topics like real estate accounting, syndication, and off-market transactions.

Pros:

  • Affordable prices and frequent discounts
  • Flexibility to study on the go with audio courses
  • 30-day money-back guarantee

Cons:

  • Study materials may not be as in-depth
  • No live instructor support
  • Limited additional study materials

Why We Chose It: 360 Training Real Estate School offers affordable pricing and comprehensive content, making it an attractive option for students on a budget. The courses are designed to be straightforward and easy to understand, providing a solid foundation for exam preparation.

Overview: 360 Training offers basic and premium course packages, both of which are fully online and can be accessed anytime. The courses are text-based and come with customer support available seven days a week. While the materials are simple and easy to understand, some students have reported issues with the software and customer service.

Why 360 Training is Great for Pre-Licensing: 360 Training’s pre-licensing courses are available for various states and include all necessary coursework to meet state requirements. The courses are structured to be self-paced, allowing students to complete them on their own schedule. Additionally, 360 Training provides exam prep courses and practice tests to help students prepare for the state licensing exam. They also offer a study planner tool to help students organize their study schedule effectively.

User Experiences and Feedback: 360 Training Real Estate School is noted for its budget-friendly pricing and straightforward course design. The self-paced format is particularly beneficial for students who need flexibility in their study schedules. The text-based materials, while simple, are comprehensive and cover all necessary topics for the licensing exams. The school also offers ongoing customer support to assist students throughout their course.

Pre-Licensing

Price Starts at: $89
Basic Package Features: To earn your real estate license in Florida, you must complete 63 hours of pre-license education on essential topics with an approved provider like AgentCampus by 360training. This Basic Package covers all FREC requirements, including modules on Real Estate Business, License Law, Agency Relationships, Brokers, Fair Housing, Property Rights, Deeds and Titles, Contracts, Mortgages, and Closings.
Upgrade Packages: The Premium Package includes an Exam Prep course with practice tests and a money-back guarantee.

Exam Prep

Price Starts at: $79
Basic Package Features: Not applicable as the Premium course is the same price, no point even looking at the basic.
Upgrade Packages: This course includes condensed study guides with essential information for the Florida Sales Associate Exam. It offers unlimited practice exams, grouped by subject areas, with feedback on each question to help you understand the correct answers and identify areas needing more study.

Continuing Education

Price Starts at: $5 Each, Only Individual Courses Available
Basic Package Features: Florida continuing education for real estate includes a 3-hour Business Ethics course every two years, Florida Asset Management, Florida Contracts (to prevent transaction errors), and Florida Fair Housing (for compliance with fair housing laws). These courses ensure professionals meet state requirements.
Upgrade Packages: None

Learning Formats: 360Training offers self-paced online courses that can be accessed 24/7 from any device. The courses are designed to be engaging with a mix of text, videos, images, and quizzes to enhance understanding and retention?.

Course Access: Students have access to their courses for a full year, providing ample time to complete the material at their own pace?.

Refund Policy: 360Training offers a 72-hour refund policy, allowing students to cancel their purchase and receive a full refund within this period if they are not satisfied?.

Exam Preparation: The platform includes exam prep materials such as practice exams, a real estate math course, and study guides to help students prepare effectively for their licensing exams. Additionally, they offer proctored final exams for states that require it? ?.

Student Support: Customer support is available seven days a week via live chat and toll-free phone calls to assist students with any issues they might encounter.

Additional Resources: The platform provides a wide range of elective courses for continuing education (CE), covering topics such as tax-favorable real estate transactions and client-focused communications. These courses are available in 32 states?.

Pros:

  • Affordable course options
  • Flexibility to study at your own pace
  • Customer support available seven days a week

Cons:

Our Review Process:

At SmartAgentAlliance, our goal is to provide accurate and helpful reviews of online real estate schools to assist prospective real estate agents in making informed decisions. Our review process involves several key steps:

Research and Data Collection:

We begin by gathering comprehensive information about each real estate school, including course offerings, pricing, accreditation, and user reviews. This involves visiting the schools’ websites, reading third-party reviews, and gathering feedback from current and former students.

Evaluation Criteria:

Our evaluation is based on several important criteria, including:

  • Course Quality: We assess the content and structure of the courses to ensure they cover all necessary topics comprehensively and are easy to follow.
  • Accessibility: We evaluate the flexibility and accessibility of the courses, including the availability of mobile apps and study materials.
  • Pass Rates: We consider the pass rates of students who have completed the courses and taken the licensing exam.
  • Student Support: We look at the level of support provided to students, including access to instructors, customer service, and additional resources like study guides and practice exams.
  • Pricing: We compare the cost of the courses relative to the value they offer, including any discounts or payment plans available.
  • User Feedback: We gather and analyze feedback from students who have taken the courses to get an understanding of their experiences and satisfaction levels.

Scoring and Ranking:

Each school is scored based on the evaluation criteria, and an overall score is calculated. The scores are then used to rank the schools, with additional considerations given to standout features that may set a school apart from the rest.

title: Compass vs Sotheby’s: Which Brokerage is Best for Realtors?
excerpt: This article will provide a comprehensive comparison between Compass vs Sotheby’s to help you determine which brokerage better aligns with your professional goals. Choosing the…
content:
This article will provide a comprehensive comparison between Compass vs Sotheby’s to help you determine which brokerage better aligns with your professional goals. Choosing the right brokerage is a critical decision for any real estate agent, as it significantly impacts their career trajectory and financial success.

Commission Splits and Fees

The commission splits and associated fees are crucial factors to consider when selecting a brokerage. Compass offers splits ranging from 60/40 to 92.5/7.5, with a no franchise/royalty fee, and some agents get a cap. Sotheby’s provides splits starting at 70/30 and going up to 90/10, also with a 6% franchise/royalty fee, and no cap.

Monthly fees also differ significantly. Compass charges $145, varying by office, whereas Sotheby’s monthly fees also vary by office. Additionally, Compass charges transaction fees up to 4%, while Sotheby’s includes transaction fees within their royalty fees. These differences can impact an agent’s net income and financial planning, making it important to consider each brokerage’s fee structure.

Income Opportunities

The potential for additional income streams can significantly enhance an agent’s financial stability and growth. Unfortunately, neither Compass nor Sotheby’s offers revenue share programs, retirement income paths, or willable income paths, limiting agents primarily to their commission-based earnings.

Training and Support

Effective training and support are vital for both new and experienced agents. Compass offers training through Compass Academy, providing structured learning opportunities. In contrast, Sotheby’s provides training that varies by office, which might lead to inconsistencies in the quality of training received. Neither brokerage offers 24/7 support, which can be crucial for agents who require ongoing assistance and professional development.

Technology and Resources

In the modern real estate landscape, leveraging technology effectively is essential. Compass is recognized for its technological advancements and robust tools available through Compass Academy. Sotheby’s also incorporates technological tools, but its offerings vary by office. Both brokerages provide valuable technological resources, but the specific tools and platforms offered may appeal differently depending on the agent’s needs.

Agent Satisfaction

Agent satisfaction is a key indicator of a brokerage’s effectiveness and overall agent happiness. Compass has a Glassdoor rating of 3.7 stars based on 2.4K reviews, indicating areas needing improvement. Sotheby’s holds a slightly higher rating of 4.0 stars from 501 reviews, suggesting relatively higher agent satisfaction. The differences in agent satisfaction can often be attributed to the varying levels of support, training, and income opportunities provided by each brokerage.

Final Thoughts on Compass vs Sotheby’s

In comparing Compass and Sotheby’s, it’s evident that each brokerage has its strengths and weaknesses. Sotheby’s offers a more traditional structure with consistent fee inclusions, making it a stable choice for many agents. Compass, with its advanced technology and structured training through Compass Academy, appeals to those who prioritize continuous learning and modern tools.

For a more detailed comparison and to find the best brokerage for your needs, check out our brokerage comparison tool.

When making your decision, consider your specific needs and career objectives. Evaluate the advantages and disadvantages carefully to choose the brokerage that best fits your professional aspirations. Whether you prioritize comprehensive support and training or seek a brokerage with a more conventional setup, your decision should align with your long-term career goals and preferences.

title: eXp Realty vs Fathom Realty: An Honest Comparison for Agents in 2026
excerpt: Comparing eXp Realty vs Fathom Financial Strength Agent Earnings Agent Fees Support Cloud Based Brokerage eXp Realty Fathom Realty Founded 2009 2010 GlassDoor Rating X/5…
content:
eXp Realty and Fathom Realty are both cloud-based brokerages that challenge the traditional franchise model. Fathom was one of the early flat-fee brokerages, originally offering a simple 100% commission model with a per-transaction fee. The company has since evolved, now offering three distinct commission plans including a revenue share option that directly competes with eXp’s model.

Agents comparing these two are often looking at the same category of brokerage – low-cost, cloud-based, technology-forward – and trying to determine which one offers the better overall value. The details matter more here than in comparisons with traditional brokerages, because the cost structures are closer and the differences are in the specifics.

This is a full breakdown of real numbers – commission plans, every fee, total annual cost, revenue share structures, and everything else that affects what you actually keep. Both brokerages have strengths. The right choice depends on which plan fits your production level and what you value beyond the commission split.

Table of Contents

Commission Structure
Total Annual Cost at Different Production Levels
Revenue Share vs Revenue Share
Training and Professional Development
Technology and Tools
Culture and Work Environment
Stock, Equity, and Wealth Building
Who Should Choose eXp Realty
Who Should Choose Fathom Realty
The Bottom Line
Frequently Asked Questions

Commission Structure

Both brokerages offer low-cost, agent-friendly commission structures. The key differences are in the plan options, caps, and post-cap fees.

eXp Realty

Every eXp agent in every market operates under the same structure:

  • 80/20 split until you reach the annual cap
  • $16,000 cap – once you have paid $16K to the brokerage, you earn 100% commission for the rest of your anniversary year
  • No franchise or royalty fees – the 80/20 split is the only commission-based cost
  • ICON Agent Program – agents who cap and meet production and cultural benchmarks can earn their full $16K cap back in eXp stock

There is no negotiation, no variation by office, and no different deal for the agent down the hall. Every agent knows exactly what they will pay before they join.

Fathom Realty

Fathom underwent a major model overhaul and now offers three distinct commission plans:

Max Plan (100% commission):

  • 100/0 split – you keep 100% of your commission
  • $9,000 cap on the annual fee
  • $465 per transaction before cap; $165 per transaction after cap
  • $250 additional fee per $500K over $500K in transaction value
  • No revenue share eligibility

Share Plan (with revenue share):

  • 88/12 split
  • $12,000 cap
  • Post-cap: $165 per transaction
  • Revenue share eligible (5-level structure)

Concierge Plan:

  • 80/20 split
  • Additional support and services included

Fathom’s model has become more complex than its original flat-fee approach. The Max plan offers the lowest split (100/0) but has high transaction fees and no revenue share. The Share plan is more comparable to eXp, with a split-based model and revenue share eligibility.

A critical detail: Fathom’s transaction fees are substantially higher than eXp’s. At $465 per transaction (Max plan before cap) versus eXp’s $25 per transaction, the per-deal cost difference is significant for agents doing volume.

Total Annual Cost at Different Production Levels

eXp Realty Fee Schedule

Fathom Realty Fee Schedule (Max Plan)

What an Agent Producing $250,000 in GCI Actually Pays

Here is what an agent earning $250,000 in gross commission income across roughly 25 transactions would pay at each brokerage.

eXp Realty:

  • Commission to brokerage (20% until $16K cap): $16,000
  • Monthly fees ($85 x 12): $1,020
  • Transaction fees ($25 x 25): $625
  • E&O ($60 x 12.5 transactions, capped at $750): $750
  • Total cost: $18,395
  • Net to agent: $231,605 (92.6%)

Fathom Max Plan:

  • Commission to brokerage: $0 (100% split)
  • Annual fee: $700
  • Transaction fees (assuming ~19 deals to reach $9K cap at $465/tx = $8,835, then 6 deals post-cap at $165/tx = $990): $9,825
  • E&O ($35 x 25): $875
  • Total cost: $11,400
  • Net to agent: $238,600 (95.4%)

Fathom Share Plan:

  • Commission to brokerage (12% until $12K cap): $12,000
  • Annual fee: $700
  • Post-cap transaction fees ($165 x remaining deals): ~$2,145
  • E&O ($35 x 25): $875
  • Total cost: ~$15,720
  • Net to agent: ~$234,280 (93.7%)

At $250K GCI, Fathom’s Max plan is less expensive than eXp by approximately $6,745. The Share plan is less expensive by approximately $2,425. However, these comparisons do not account for the value of eXp’s ICON program (which can return the full $16K cap in stock), revenue share income, or the differences in tools and support between the two brokerages.

Revenue Share vs Revenue Share

Both brokerages offer revenue share programs, but the structures and payouts differ significantly.

eXp Revenue Share

eXp’s revenue share is funded by 50% of the company dollar (the 20% retained from agent commissions before capping). It operates across seven tiers:

Maximum revenue share per capping agent across all seven tiers: $16,000/year.

In 2024, eXp distributed more than $170 million in revenue share. Since 2015, total payouts have exceeded $889 million.

eXp revenue share is willable to heirs after vesting (60% at 3 years, 80% at 4 years, 100% at 5 years) and continues as retirement income while your license stays with eXp.

Fathom Revenue Share

Fathom introduced revenue share as part of its model overhaul. Key details:

  • Only available on the Share plan (88/12 split) – not available on the Max (100/0) or Concierge plans
  • 5-level structure with payouts of 35%/25%/20%/15%/5% of company dollar
  • Retirement and willable income paths are uncertain – the research report flags these as LOW confidence with unclear documentation

Fathom’s revenue share program is newer and less proven than eXp’s. eXp has paid out nearly $900 million in revenue share over a decade. Fathom’s program is still being established, and the long-term payout history does not yet exist.

The critical trade-off: to access Fathom’s revenue share, you must choose the Share plan (88/12 split, $12K cap) instead of the Max plan (100/0, $9K cap). This means you pay more in commission costs to be eligible for revenue share. At eXp, every agent on the standard plan is automatically eligible for revenue share.

Training and Professional Development

eXp Realty

eXp runs one of the largest agent training programs in the industry through eXp University:

  • 50+ live training sessions per week covering new agent fundamentals through advanced marketing and investing
  • Full on-demand course library accessible 24/7
  • Mentor program pairing new agents with experienced mentors for their first transactions (required, not optional)
  • Fast Start program for new agents and Kick Start for experienced agents joining eXp
  • All training is included at no additional cost

Fathom Realty

Fathom has expanded its training offerings:

  • 600+ on-demand courses covering sales, marketing, and business development
  • Fathom’s training library has grown significantly from its earlier limited offering
  • No mandatory mentor program

eXp’s training program is larger (50+ live sessions per week) and includes a mandatory mentor program that Fathom does not offer. Fathom’s 600+ on-demand courses represent a meaningful improvement over its earlier training capabilities.

Technology and Tools

eXp Realty

  • eXp World – virtual campus for meetings, training, collaboration, and broker access
  • kvCORE CRM – included for every agent (a platform that costs $300 to $500/month independently)
  • Skyslope for transaction management
  • IDX website included for every agent
  • Marketing Center with customizable templates, social media content, and branding tools
  • Revenos – a dedicated company team focused on generating and distributing leads to eXp agents

Fathom Realty

  • Fathom’s proprietary technology platform – intelliAgent for transaction management
  • CRM tools available but less comprehensive than kvCORE
  • Marketing tools and templates
  • Agent websites available

eXp’s technology stack is more comprehensive, particularly kvCORE (worth $300-$500/month independently) and the eXp World virtual platform. Fathom’s tools are functional but more basic. The technology gap is one of the areas where eXp’s slightly higher cost translates into tangible value.

Culture and Work Environment

eXp: Cloud-First, Location-Independent

eXp agents work from anywhere. Collaboration happens through eXp World, virtual meetups, and regional events. eXp provides free access to Regus business lounges worldwide. With over 80,000 agents, eXp has a large, active community with extensive networking opportunities.

Fathom: Cloud-Based, Lean Operation

Fathom is also cloud-based with no physical offices. The brokerage operates with a leaner model than eXp – fewer agents, fewer corporate employees, and a more stripped-down approach. Fathom attracts agents who want the lowest possible fees and are comfortable operating independently with minimal brokerage infrastructure.

The community at Fathom is smaller than eXp’s, which means fewer networking opportunities and a smaller peer group. For agents who are fully self-sufficient and just want a low-cost place to hang their license, this is not a drawback. For agents who value community, collaboration, and a large support network, eXp’s scale is an advantage.

Stock, Equity, and Wealth Building

eXp Realty

eXp is publicly traded on NASDAQ (EXPI) and offers agents multiple paths to stock ownership:

  • ICON Agent Program – agents who cap and meet production and cultural benchmarks can earn their full $16,000 cap back in eXp stock
  • Agent Equity Program – agents can choose to receive a portion of their commission in stock at a discount
  • Top Agent Bonus – $16K ($8K immediate + $8K vesting over 3 years) for qualifying top producers

Fathom Realty

Fathom Holdings is publicly traded on NASDAQ (FTHM). However, Fathom does not offer agent stock awards, an ICON-equivalent program, or a production-based equity program. You can purchase Fathom stock on the open market, but there is no company program to help agents earn shares through production.

This is a significant differentiator. eXp’s ICON program can return the full $16K cap in stock, effectively making the cost of doing business at eXp zero for qualifying agents. Fathom has no equivalent program.

Who Should Choose eXp Realty

eXp tends to be the stronger fit for agents who:

  • Want the best combination of low cost and comprehensive tools – kvCORE, eXp World, and the full tech stack provide more value than Fathom’s tools
  • Want to build significant revenue share income – eXp’s 7-tier, $889M+ proven program is more established and deeper than Fathom’s 5-level program
  • Want stock equity through production – the ICON program can effectively make eXp free for capping agents
  • Value a large agent community – 80,000+ agents provide extensive networking, collaboration, and learning opportunities
  • Want a mandatory mentor program – particularly valuable for newer agents
  • Want proven retirement and willable income – eXp’s vesting and willability are well-documented; Fathom’s are uncertain

Who Should Choose Fathom Realty

Fathom tends to be the stronger fit for agents who:

  • Want the absolute lowest transaction cost – Fathom’s Max plan (100/0 with $9K cap) has lower total costs than eXp at most production levels
  • Are fully self-sufficient – you generate your own leads, have your own CRM, and do not need extensive brokerage tools or community
  • Do not plan to build a revenue share network – if passive income is not a priority, the Max plan’s lower cost is a straightforward advantage
  • Prefer simplicity – you just want a low-cost place to hang your license without the larger ecosystem
  • Are not focused on stock equity – without a program like ICON, the lower base cost is more relevant than potential equity awards

Fathom serves a real need for agents who want the lowest possible brokerage cost and are willing to provide their own tools and support. It is a legitimate option for experienced, self-sufficient agents who view the brokerage purely as a license home.

The Bottom Line

This is a closer comparison than most in this series. At $250K GCI, Fathom’s Max plan costs approximately $6,745 less than eXp. The Share plan costs approximately $2,425 less.

But the cost comparison alone misses the bigger picture. eXp offers kvCORE (worth $300-$500/month), a 7-tier revenue share program that has paid $889M+, the ICON program that can return your entire cap in stock, a mandatory mentor program, 50+ live training sessions per week, and a community of 80,000+ agents. Fathom offers lower base costs with a leaner infrastructure.

For agents who factor in the ICON program, the cost comparison flips entirely – an ICON-qualifying eXp agent effectively pays $0 in net cap cost, making eXp less expensive than even Fathom’s Max plan. For agents building revenue share networks, eXp’s proven, 7-tier, willable program with $889M in historical payouts is substantially more valuable than Fathom’s newer 5-level program.

For agents who simply want the lowest out-of-pocket cost and do not plan to leverage ICON, revenue share, or the broader eXp ecosystem, Fathom’s Max plan saves money. The right choice depends on whether you view a brokerage as just a license home or as a platform for long-term wealth building.

Frequently Asked Questions

Is eXp Realty better than Fathom for new agents?

For new agents, eXp provides more support. eXp’s mandatory mentor program, 50+ weekly live training sessions, and kvCORE CRM give new agents tools and guidance that Fathom’s leaner model does not match. Fathom’s 600+ on-demand courses are a resource, but there is no mandatory mentorship. New agents who need structure and support will benefit more from eXp.

Is Fathom cheaper than eXp?

In pure out-of-pocket costs, yes. Fathom’s Max plan (100/0, $9K cap) costs approximately $6,745 less per year than eXp at $250K GCI. However, if you qualify for eXp’s ICON program (earning your $16K cap back in stock), eXp’s effective cost is $0 – making it less expensive than Fathom. The total value equation depends on whether you leverage eXp’s equity and revenue share programs.

Does Fathom have revenue share?

Yes, but only on the Share plan (88/12 split, $12K cap). Fathom’s revenue share is a 5-level structure with payouts of 35%/25%/20%/15%/5%. It is not available on the Max (100/0) or Concierge plans. eXp’s revenue share is a 7-tier program available to all agents, with $889M+ in historical payouts and documented willability and retirement provisions.

What are Fathom’s transaction fees?

Fathom’s Max plan charges $465 per transaction before cap and $165 per transaction after cap. There is also a $250 fee per $500K of transaction value over $500K. Plus $35 per transaction for E&O. These fees are significantly higher than eXp’s $25 per transaction and $40 per transaction E&O (capped at $500/year).

Can you earn stock at Fathom?

Fathom Holdings is publicly traded (NASDAQ: FTHM), but there is no agent stock award program equivalent to eXp’s ICON or Agent Equity programs. You can buy Fathom stock on the open market, but there is no company program for agents to earn shares through production.

Is eXp revenue share a pyramid scheme?

No. Revenue share at eXp is funded by actual real estate transactions, not by agent recruitment fees. When an agent in your network closes a deal and pays the 20% company dollar, half of that flows into the revenue share pool. No money is paid unless real estate is sold.

Which brokerage has better technology – eXp or Fathom?

eXp has a more comprehensive technology stack. kvCORE CRM (worth $300-$500/month independently), eXp World virtual platform, Skyslope transaction management, IDX websites, Marketing Center, and Revenos lead generation are all included. Fathom provides functional tools but at a more basic level. For agents who rely on brokerage-provided technology, eXp offers significantly more value.

title: eXp Realty vs LPT Realty: An Honest Comparison for Agents in 2026
