content:
LPT Realty has quickly become one of the most talked-about cloud-based brokerages in real estate. With two distinct commission plans, a 7-tier revenue share program, and stock awards for top producers, LPT positions itself as a serious alternative for agents who want low costs and passive income potential. If you’re comparing LPT Realty to eXp Realty, you’re likely weighing two models that look similar on the surface but differ significantly in scale, tools, and long-term wealth-building potential.

Both brokerages operate on a cloud-first model with no physical offices, both offer revenue share programs, and both provide stock incentives. But the details matter. eXp Realty has over 90,000 agents worldwide, a publicly traded stock (EXPI on Nasdaq), and has paid out more than $889 million in revenue share. LPT Realty is newer and growing, with non-publicly traded stock and a revenue share program still building momentum. The question isn’t just which costs less today – it’s which one creates more value over a full career.

This comparison breaks down every major category using verified 2026 data so you can make an informed decision. We’ll cover commission structures, total annual costs, revenue share programs, training, technology, culture, and equity opportunities at both brokerages.

What We’ll Cover

  • Commission Structure
  • Total Annual Cost to the Agent
  • Revenue Share vs Revenue Share
  • Training and Education
  • Technology and Tools
  • Culture and Community
  • Stock and Equity Opportunities
  • Who Should Choose eXp Realty
  • Who Should Choose LPT Realty
  • The Bottom Line
  • Frequently Asked Questions

Commission Structure

LPT Realty offers two distinct commission plans, giving agents flexibility based on their production level and business model. This is one of LPT’s key differentiators – you can choose the plan that best fits your transaction volume.

LPT Realty Commission Plans

Brokerage Partner Plan (80/20 split):

  • 80/20 commission split with a $15,000 annual cap
  • After cap: 100% commission for the remainder of the anniversary year
  • $195 transaction fee on every transaction where GCI is $2,500+ (continues after cap)
  • $500 annual fee (includes E&O insurance)
  • Optional LPT Plus package: $89/month for additional tools
  • 0% franchise or royalty fee

Business Builder Plan (flat fee per transaction):

  • Flat $500 per transaction (no percentage-based split)
  • $5,000 annual cap – after 10 transactions, no more commission fees
  • $195 transaction fee on every transaction where GCI is $2,500+ (continues after cap)
  • $500 annual fee (includes E&O insurance)
  • Optional LPT Plus package: $149/month for additional tools
  • 0% franchise or royalty fee

eXp Realty Commission Structure

eXp Realty uses a single, standardized commission plan for all agents:

  • 80/20 commission split with a $16,000 annual cap
  • After cap: 100% commission for the remainder of the anniversary year
  • $250 per-transaction fee after cap (max 20 transactions), then $75 sponsor fee per transaction
  • $85/month technology fee ($175/month for first 6 months for new agents)
  • Approximately $40-65/month for E&O insurance
  • 0% franchise or royalty fee

At first glance, LPT’s Business Builder plan looks significantly cheaper than eXp’s structure. But eXp’s ICON Agent program can rebate the entire $16,000 cap as company stock when production and cultural milestones are met – effectively making eXp a flat-fee model for top producers as well.

Total Annual Cost to the Agent

Here’s how the numbers play out for an agent producing $250,000 in GCI across 20 transactions in a year. We’ll compare both LPT plans against eXp.

LPT Brokerage Partner Plan (80/20) at $250K GCI

Fee CategoryAmount
Commission to LPT (20% until $15K cap)$15,000
Annual Fee$500
Transaction Fees (20 x $195)$3,900
E&O Insurance$0 (included)
Total Annual Cost$19,400

LPT Business Builder Plan (flat fee) at $250K GCI

Fee CategoryAmount
Flat Fee to LPT ($500/tx x 10 to cap)$5,000
Annual Fee$500
Transaction Fees (20 x $195)$3,900
E&O Insurance$0 (included)
Total Annual Cost$9,400

eXp Realty at $250K GCI

Fee CategoryAmount
Commission to eXp (20% until $16K cap)$16,000
Monthly Tech Fee ($85 x 12)$1,020
E&O Insurance ($60/txn, $750 cap)$750
Total Annual Cost$17,770

The Comparison

LPT Brokerage Partner vs eXp: eXp saves you approximately $1,780 per year. The plans are structurally similar (both 80/20), but LPT’s lower cap ($15K vs $16K) is offset by the $3,900 in transaction fees that continue after cap.

LPT Business Builder vs eXp: LPT saves you approximately $8,220 per year in direct costs. This is the plan that makes LPT attractive for high-volume producers who want the lowest possible overhead.

But direct costs don’t tell the whole story. eXp’s ICON Agent program can rebate the entire $16,000 cap as EXPI stock, and eXp agents receive publicly traded stock that can be sold on the open market. When you factor in ICON eligibility, revenue share scale, and stock liquidity, the total value equation shifts significantly.

Revenue Share vs Revenue Share

This is where the comparison gets interesting. Unlike most traditional brokerages, both eXp and LPT offer revenue share programs. The question isn’t whether you can earn passive income – it’s which program gives you the better long-term return.

eXp Realty Revenue Share

eXp’s revenue share program distributes a percentage of the company dollar (the brokerage’s portion of each agent’s commission) across seven tiers of sponsorship:

TierPercentage of Company DollarMax Agents in Tier
Tier 1 (direct sponsors)50% (3.5% of GCI)Unlimited
Tier 210% (0.7% of GCI)Unlimited
Tier 35% (0.35% of GCI)Unlimited
Tier 45% (0.35% of GCI)Unlimited
Tier 55% (0.35% of GCI)Unlimited
Tier 62.5% (0.175% of GCI)Unlimited
Tier 72.5% (0.175% of GCI)Unlimited

eXp has paid out over $889 million in revenue share since the program’s inception. The program is fully willable and can be passed to heirs, creating a genuine retirement income path.

LPT Realty Revenue Share

LPT offers a 7-tier revenue share program that distributes 50% of company dollars across the sponsorship tree. The program is willable starting at 3 years, with a vesting schedule:

  • Year 3: 60% willable
  • Year 4: 80% willable
  • Year 5+: 100% willable

Key Differences

Scale and track record: eXp’s revenue share program has been running since 2009 with over 90,000 agents contributing to the pool. More agents means more company dollars being generated, which means larger potential payouts across all seven tiers. LPT’s program is newer with a smaller agent base, so the earning potential is currently more limited.

Willability: eXp’s revenue share is fully willable from day one. LPT requires a 3-year vesting period before the income becomes willable, reaching 100% only at year 5. If something happens in your first three years at LPT, your revenue share organization doesn’t transfer to your heirs.

Proven payouts: eXp’s $889M+ in total revenue share payouts is a verified, public number. LPT’s total payouts are not publicly disclosed, which makes it harder to evaluate the program’s real-world earning potential.

Both programs reward agents for attracting other agents to the brokerage. But eXp’s combination of scale, immediate willability, and a decade-plus track record makes it the more proven vehicle for building long-term passive income.

Training and Education

LPT Realty Training

LPT Realty provides a solid training ecosystem that includes:

  • Daily live training sessions plus virtual training events
  • On-demand training library with recorded content
  • Monday Motivation sessions for mindset and goal-setting
  • Peer-to-peer learning within agent communities

Training at LPT is rated medium-high confidence in our research, with agents generally reporting positive experiences with the daily live format and the accessibility of on-demand content.

eXp Realty Training

eXp Realty operates one of the most comprehensive training platforms in the industry:

  • eXp University: 50+ hours of live training per week across every specialization
  • eXp Collaborate: Company-wide collaboration platform connecting 90,000+ agents
  • Mentorship program: New agents are paired with experienced mentors for their first transactions
  • On-demand library: Thousands of recorded sessions searchable by topic
  • ICON Agent mentorship: Top producers share strategies through dedicated training tracks

The scale difference matters here. With 90,000+ agents, eXp can offer specialized training tracks for luxury, commercial, investment, new construction, and dozens of other niches. The sheer volume of expertise available on the platform is hard to replicate at a smaller scale.

Technology and Tools

LPT Realty Technology

LPT Realty’s technology offering includes:

  • Basic tech tools included with the annual fee
  • Optional LPT Plus package ($89-149/month) for enhanced tools
  • 24/7 agent support via phone, chat, and email
  • Cloud-based operations with no physical office requirements

One thing worth noting: LPT’s base package is lean on tech tools. The enhanced features require the optional LPT Plus subscription, which adds $89/month (Brokerage Partner) or $149/month (Business Builder) to your costs. If you need a robust CRM and marketing suite, that subscription effectively becomes a required cost rather than an optional one.

eXp Realty Technology

eXp includes enterprise-grade technology as part of the standard $85/month tech fee:

  • kvCORE: Full CRM with IDX website, lead generation, automated follow-up, and transaction management (valued at $500+/month as a standalone product)
  • eXp Collaborate: Company-wide communication and collaboration platform
  • Skyslope: Transaction management and digital signatures
  • Custom agent website: IDX-enabled personal website included
  • Marketing center: Customizable marketing materials and templates

The kvCORE platform alone is a major differentiator. It’s a $500+/month enterprise CRM that eXp agents get included in their $85 monthly fee. At LPT, you’d need to source and pay for your own CRM separately unless you subscribe to LPT Plus, and even then the tools may not match kvCORE’s depth.

Culture and Community

LPT Realty Culture

LPT Realty cultivates a supportive, growth-oriented culture focused on:

  • Agent-first philosophy with accessible leadership
  • Monday Motivation calls that build community
  • Active social media communities and peer networking
  • Emphasis on work-life balance and entrepreneurial freedom

LPT’s smaller size can be an advantage culturally – agents often report feeling less like a number and more like part of a tight-knit community. Leadership tends to be more accessible when the organization is smaller.

eXp Realty Culture

eXp Realty has built one of the most distinctive cultures in real estate:

  • eXp Collaborate: Always-on platform connecting agents globally for instant support and collaboration
  • EXPCON: Annual conference bringing thousands of agents together for training and networking
  • Revenue share alignment: Because sponsors earn when their recruits succeed, there’s a built-in incentive to help others grow
  • Agent-owned mentality: Stock ownership gives agents a stake in the company’s success

eXp’s culture benefits from scale – with 90,000+ agents, you can find specialists in virtually any niche, market, or business model. The revenue share structure creates genuine alignment where experienced agents actively invest time in helping newer agents succeed because their income depends on it.

LPT shares this alignment through its own revenue share program, but eXp’s decade-plus head start means the collaborative culture is deeper and more established.

Stock and Equity Opportunities

LPT Realty Stock Awards

LPT Realty offers stock awards at three tiers based on production and recruiting:

  • Silver: 100-140 shares
  • Gold: 1,000-1,400 shares
  • Black: Up to 3,150 shares

Critical detail: LPT Realty stock is not publicly traded. This means there’s no open market to sell your shares, no transparent daily pricing, and limited liquidity. The value of your stock awards depends entirely on LPT’s future – either through an eventual IPO, acquisition, or internal buyback program.

eXp Realty Stock Opportunities

eXp Realty (EXPI) is publicly traded on the Nasdaq exchange, giving agents multiple ways to earn and trade real equity:

  • ICON Agent Award: Earn back your entire $16,000 cap as EXPI stock by meeting production and cultural benchmarks
  • Agent Equity Program: Purchase EXPI stock at a 10% discount through payroll deduction
  • Sustainable Equity Plan: Receive stock grants at key milestones (first transaction, annual anniversary, capping)
  • Revenue share in stock: Option to receive revenue share payments as EXPI stock at a 5% bonus

The difference in stock liquidity is significant. EXPI shares can be sold on any trading day through any brokerage account. You can see the price in real time, set limit orders, and convert shares to cash whenever you choose. With LPT’s non-publicly traded shares, you’re holding a private asset with no guaranteed exit strategy until the company goes public or is acquired.

For agents building long-term wealth, the ability to convert stock awards into actual dollars on a predictable timeline is a meaningful advantage.

Who Should Choose eXp Realty

eXp Realty is the stronger choice if you:

  • Want proven revenue share at scale: $889M+ paid out across a 90,000+ agent network with immediate willability
  • Value stock liquidity: EXPI trades on the Nasdaq – you can sell shares whenever you want at transparent market prices
  • Need enterprise-grade tools included: kvCORE CRM, IDX website, and marketing suite are all part of the standard package
  • Are targeting ICON status: Meeting ICON benchmarks rebates your entire $16,000 cap as stock, making eXp effectively cheaper than LPT’s Business Builder plan
  • Want the deepest training library: 50+ hours of live training weekly across every specialization and niche
  • Are building a team or organization: eXp’s scale means more agents to recruit from, more niches to serve, and more collaboration opportunities

Who Should Choose LPT Realty

LPT Realty could be a fit if you:

  • Want the absolute lowest direct costs: The Business Builder plan at $9,400/year is hard to beat for pure out-of-pocket savings
  • Are a high-volume producer who doesn’t need CRM tools: If you already have your own tech stack, the Business Builder’s flat $500/transaction model keeps costs predictable
  • Prefer a smaller, newer community: Some agents prefer the tight-knit feel of a growing brokerage over a large established one
  • Believe in LPT’s long-term growth story: If LPT eventually goes public, early stock awards could appreciate significantly
  • Want E&O insurance included: LPT bundles E&O into the $500 annual fee, simplifying your cost structure
  • Value daily live training format: LPT’s daily sessions with Monday Motivation may match your preferred learning style

The Bottom Line

LPT Realty and eXp Realty are both legitimate cloud-based brokerages with revenue share programs, stock awards, and agent-centric models. The comparison comes down to what matters most to you: lowest possible direct costs or maximum total value.

LPT’s Business Builder plan is genuinely cheaper – saving roughly $8,220 per year in direct costs compared to eXp at $250K GCI. That’s real money, and for agents who already have their own CRM, marketing tools, and don’t care about stock liquidity, it’s a compelling proposition.

But eXp’s advantages compound over time. A publicly traded stock you can sell tomorrow is worth more than a private stock you might be able to sell someday. A revenue share program with $889M+ in proven payouts across 90,000+ agents is a different proposition than a newer program still building scale. An ICON-eligible agent at eXp who gets their $16,000 cap rebated as stock is effectively paying less than LPT’s Business Builder plan while also accumulating liquid equity.

The pattern we see most often: agents who focus purely on minimizing today’s costs choose LPT. Agents who focus on maximizing career-long wealth – through stock appreciation, revenue share compounding, and ICON eligibility – choose eXp. Both are valid strategies. The right choice depends on your time horizon and what you’re optimizing for.

Frequently Asked Questions

Which LPT Realty plan is most comparable to eXp’s commission structure?

The Brokerage Partner plan (80/20 split, $15K cap) is most similar to eXp’s structure (80/20 split, $16K cap). At $250K GCI, eXp actually costs about $1,780 less than this LPT plan because LPT’s $195 per-transaction fees continue after cap and add up quickly. LPT’s Business Builder plan (flat $500/transaction, $5K cap) is a completely different model that’s cheaper in direct costs but doesn’t include comparable tools.

Can I sell LPT Realty stock the same way I can sell eXp stock?

No. eXp Realty (EXPI) is publicly traded on the Nasdaq, meaning you can sell shares through any brokerage account on any trading day at transparent market prices. LPT Realty stock is not publicly traded, so there’s no open market to sell shares. You’d need to wait for an IPO, acquisition, or internal buyback program. This is a fundamental difference in how the two stock programs create value for agents.

How do eXp and LPT revenue share programs compare?

Both offer 7-tier revenue share programs, and both distribute a portion of company dollars to sponsors. The key differences are scale and willability. eXp has paid out $889M+ to agents across a 90,000+ agent network, with immediate willability from day one. LPT’s program requires a 3-year vesting period before revenue share becomes willable (60% at year 3, 80% at year 4, 100% at year 5).

Does LPT Realty include a CRM like eXp’s kvCORE?

Not in the base package. LPT’s standard plan includes basic tools with the $500 annual fee. For enhanced technology, you’d need the optional LPT Plus subscription at $89/month (Brokerage Partner) or $149/month (Business Builder). eXp includes kvCORE – a full enterprise CRM with IDX website, lead generation, and automated follow-up – as part of the standard $85/month tech fee. If you need a CRM at LPT, factor that additional cost into your comparison.

What is eXp’s ICON Agent program and how does it change the cost comparison?

ICON is eXp’s top-producer recognition program. Agents who meet production benchmarks and cultural contributions receive their entire $16,000 cap back as EXPI stock. For an ICON agent, the effective annual cost drops to just the monthly tech fee ($1,020) plus E&O ($750) – roughly $1,770 total. That’s significantly less than even LPT’s Business Builder plan at $9,400, and the agent also receives $16,000 in publicly traded stock.

Is LPT Realty revenue share willable like eXp’s?

Yes, but with conditions. LPT’s revenue share becomes willable on a vesting schedule: 60% at 3 years, 80% at 4 years, and 100% at 5 years. eXp’s revenue share is fully willable from day one with no vesting period. If building a legacy income stream for your family is a priority, this vesting difference matters – especially in the early years of building your organization.

Which brokerage is better for a new agent?

eXp Realty generally provides more support infrastructure for new agents. The mentorship program pairs new agents with experienced mentors, eXp University offers 50+ hours of live training weekly, kvCORE provides a professional CRM from day one, and the 90,000+ agent network means you can find help with virtually any question or situation. LPT offers daily training and 24/7 support, but the smaller agent base means fewer specialists to learn from and a less comprehensive mentorship structure.

title: Compass vs Corcoran: Which is Best for Realtors in 2026?
excerpt: This article will provide a comprehensive comparison between Compass vs Corcoran to help you determine which brokerage better aligns with your professional goals. Choosing the…
content:
This article will provide a comprehensive comparison between Compass vs Corcoran to help you determine which brokerage better aligns with your professional goals. Choosing the right brokerage is a critical decision for any real estate agent, as it significantly impacts their career trajectory and financial success.

Commission Splits and Fees

The commission splits and associated fees are crucial factors to consider when selecting a brokerage. Compass offers splits ranging from 60/40 to 92.5/7.5, with a no franchise/royalty fee. Corcoran provides splits starting at 50/50 and going up to 70/30, with a 6% franchise/royalty fee as well. Furthermore, Coompass does technically have a production cap, though not always, this gets a bit murky, Corcoran on the other hand doesnt at all.

Monthly fees also differ significantly. Compass charges $145, varying by office, whereas Corcoran’s fees also vary by office but include the franchise fee within their royalty fees. Additionally, Compass charges transaction fees up to 4%, while Corcoran includes transaction fees within their royalty fees. These differences can impact an agent’s net income and financial planning, making it important to consider each brokerage’s fee structure.

Income Opportunities

The potential for additional income streams can significantly enhance an agent’s financial stability and growth. Unfortunately, neither Compass nor Corcoran offers revenue share programs, retirement income paths, or willable income paths, limiting agents primarily to their commission-based earnings.

Training and Support

Effective training and support are vital for both new and experienced agents. Compass offers training through Compass Academy, providing structured learning opportunities. In contrast, Corcoran provides training that varies by office, which might lead to inconsistencies in the quality of training received. Neither brokerage offers 24/7 support, which can be crucial for agents who require ongoing assistance and professional development.

Technology and Resources

In the modern real estate landscape, leveraging technology effectively is essential. Compass is recognized for its technological advancements and robust tools available through Compass Academy. On the other hand, Corcoran, while incorporating some technological tools, still focuses heavily on traditional methods. Both brokerages provide valuable technological resources, but the specific tools and platforms offered may appeal differently depending on the agent’s needs.

Agent Satisfaction

Agent satisfaction is a key indicator of a brokerage’s effectiveness and overall agent happiness. Corcoran boasts a high Glassdoor rating of 4.4 stars based on 321 reviews, indicating strong agent approval and satisfaction. Compass holds a lower rating of 3.7 stars from 2.4K reviews, suggesting that there may be areas needing improvement. The differences in agent satisfaction can often be attributed to the varying levels of support, training, and income opportunities provided by each brokerage.

Final Thoughts on Compass vs Corcoran

In comparing Compass and Corcoran, it’s evident that each brokerage has its strengths and weaknesses. Corcoran offers a more consistent fee structure and higher agent satisfaction, making it a stable choice for many agents. Compass, with its advanced technology and structured training through Compass Academy, appeals to those who prioritize technological integration and continuous learning.

For a more detailed comparison and to find the best brokerage for your needs, check out our brokerage comparison tool.

When making your decision, consider your specific needs and career objectives. Evaluate the advantages and disadvantages carefully to choose the brokerage that best fits your professional aspirations. Whether you prioritize comprehensive support and training or seek a brokerage with a more conventional setup, your decision should align with your long-term career goals and preferences.
